entities.name: ANAEMIA
entities.description: A medical condition characterized by a lack of healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to the body's tissues.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the burden of anaemia is being studied and addressed.

entities.name: FOOD-BASED STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches to mitigate anaemia through dietary changes and food-based interventions.

entities.name: CLINICAL STRATEGIES
entities.description: Medical interventions used to treat or mitigate anaemia.

entities.name: COMPREHENSIVE NATIONAL NUTRITION SURVEY
entities.description: A survey conducted between 2016-2018 to measure Hb concentration in children and adolescents.

entities.name: HB CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The level of hemoglobin in the blood, used as a marker for anaemia.

entities.name: HUMANS
entities.description: The population affected by anaemia, specifically children and adolescents in the context of the survey.

entities.name: VENOUS BLOOD SAMPLING
entities.description: A method of collecting blood from a vein to measure Hb levels.

entities.name: CAPILLARY BLOOD SAMPLING
entities.description: A method of collecting blood from capillaries, noted as potentially erroneous for Hb measurement.

entities.name: IRON
entities.description: The micronutrient (Fe) essential for preventing and treating anaemia.

entities.name: FOOD FORTIFICATION
entities.description: The process of adding micronutrients, like iron, to food to improve nutritional quality.

entities.name: IRON SUPPLEMENTATION
entities.description: The pharmacological administration of iron via tablets or syrups.

entities.name: WHO
entities.description: The World Health Organization, which defines the Hb threshold for anaemia.

entities.name: HB THRESHOLD
entities.description: The specific hemoglobin level used to define the presence of anaemia.
